steps.title: The y-intercept occurs at x = 0
steps.content: The y-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, which always happens when $x = 0$.

steps.title: Substitute x = 0 and evaluate
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 0$ into the function:
$$g(0) = 11\left(\frac{1}{12}\right)^0$$
Any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1, so $\left(\frac{1}{12}\right)^0 = 1$:
$$g(0) = 11 \cdot 1 = 11$$
The y-intercept is $(0, 11)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
